sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: United Way of Southern Tier reveals 2026 award winners. See list
feed.summary: The United Way of the Southern Tier announced its 2026 Community Award winners, celebrating local individuals and organizations at a dinner on April 30th at the Corning Museum of Glass. Honorees include John G. and Bobbie Ullman, Patty Howe, and Corning Credit Union for their significant community contributions. Corning Incorporated will also receive a special award recognizing its 175th anniversary.

feed.title: Conagra Brands (CAG) Q3 FY2026 Earnings: GAAP EPS $0.42 vs. $0.4
feed.summary: Conagra Brands reported its Q3 FY2026 earnings, exceeding revenue and GAAP EPS estimates with $2.8 billion and $0.42 respectively, but missed on adjusted EPS at $0.39. The company saw a return to organic net sales growth due to strong performance in its Frozen and Snacks businesses. Despite this, gross margins compressed due to cost inflation, and free cash flow decreased year-over-year, though net debt was reduced.

feed.title: Yum Brands names former Marriott executive to board of directors
feed.summary: Yum Brands has appointed Kathleen Oberg, former CFO of Marriott International, to its board of directors. Oberg retired from Marriott in March after a decade as CFO and brings a strong background in financial discipline and operational performance to Yum Brands. This move aims to strengthen the company's leadership with her extensive industry experience.

feed.title: Coca-Cola Beverages unveils America250 Commemorative Cans ​
feed.summary: Coca-Cola Beverages Florida has launched limited-edition "America250 Coca-Cola cans" to celebrate the United States' 250th anniversary. These commemorative cans, produced at the Jacksonville facility, will feature patriotic branding representing all 50 states and will be available starting April 6 across Florida. Coke Florida, a Signature Partner of America250, also plans community engagement initiatives throughout 2026 to support the national celebration.

feed.title: Green Century Funds Submits Shareholder Proposal to Skyworks Solutions
feed.summary: Green Century Funds has submitted a shareholder proposal to Skyworks Solutions, Inc., requesting the company to issue a report on how it plans to increase the scale, pace, and rigor of its greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions reduction efforts. This proposal was announced on April 3, 2026, and seeks disclosures beyond Skyworks Solutions' existing reports.

feed.title: Genuine Parts (NYSE: GPC) director receives 242-share phantom stock award
feed.summary: Genuine Parts Co. (NYSE: GPC) director Richard Cox Jr. was granted 242 shares of phantom stock, each valued at $103.52 per share. This award increases his total phantom stock holdings to 5,323 shares. The phantom stock is economically equivalent to GPC common stock and will be settled in cash or common stock based on a prior deferral election, including 52 shares acquired via a Dividend Reinvestment Plan.

feed.title: Las ETF que invierten en acciones de Spire Inc.
feed.summary: This article lists Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) that invest in shares of Spire Inc. (SR), a gas distribution company. It provides a detailed table with information on each ETF including market value, weight of SR in the fund, issuer, management style, expense ratio, assets under management (AUM), price, percentage change, relative volume, and 3-year total return. The ETFs cover various investment styles such as passive, active, mid-cap, small-cap, value, growth, and high dividend yield, offering investors diversified exposure to Spire Inc.

feed.title: VolitionRX (VNRX) FY2025 Earnings: Q4 EPS and Revenue Not Disclo
feed.summary: VolitionRX Ltd (VNRX) reported full-year 2025 revenue of $1.7 million, a 40% increase year-over-year, alongside a 17% reduction in operating expenses and a 24% improvement in operating cash burn. Despite these operational improvements, the full-year revenue significantly missed analyst estimates of $9.82 million, and Q4 EPS and revenue were not disclosed. The company highlighted strategic progress in cancer and sepsis initiatives, including a first order for Nu.Q® Cancer assays and participation in a sepsis evaluation program, but an audit's "going concern" explanatory paragraph indicates ongoing reliance on external funding.

feed.title: Washington Federal Inc stock: Earnings watch heats up amid Hold consensus
feed.summary: WaFd Bank is nearing its Q2 2026 earnings release, with investors closely watching for a rebound after prior misses. The regional bank is navigating a pivot from traditional mortgage lending to commercial real estate and business loans, driven by regulatory changes, while also maintaining a strong dividend yield and share repurchase program. Analysts currently hold a consensus "Hold" rating, awaiting Q2 results to assess the effectiveness of its "Build 2030" strategy and commercial lending growth.

feed.title: 9 Consumer Discretionary Stocks Whale Activity In Today's Session
feed.summary: This article highlights nine consumer discretionary stocks that experienced significant "whale" option trading activity during today's session. It details specific call option trades for companies like Tesla, Norwegian Cruise Line, Carvana, Home Depot, DoorDash, Restaurant Brands International, Wingstop, Ollie's Bargain Outlet, and Booking Holdings, including strike prices, expiration dates, and sentiment. The article serves as a guide for traders looking to identify potential opportunities based on large-scale options movements.

feed.title: New Burlington-built furnaces will heat-treat parts used across multiple Boeing aircraft
feed.summary: Boeing has committed $7 million to Canadian supplier Vac Aero International for two massive vacuum heat-treating furnaces, which are being built in Burlington. These furnaces will support Boeing's aerospace manufacturing operations by heat treating critical tube and duct assemblies for various aircraft, a project Vac Aero executives describe as one of the largest in the company's history. The deal not only secures Burlington's role in the global aerospace supply chain but also coincides with a 40% surge in sales for Vac Aero.

feed.title: Comcast (NASDAQ: CMCSA) EVP nets shares after 5,268 RSUs vest and tax withholding
feed.summary: Comcast EVP Daniel C. Murdock acquired 5,268 shares of Class A Common Stock from the vesting of restricted stock units. Out of these, 2,073 shares were withheld by the company to cover tax obligations at a price of $27.93 per share, which was not an open-market sale. Following these transactions, Murdock directly holds 62,362.0497 shares of Comcast Class A Common Stock.

feed.title: U.S. Gold delivers Feasibility Study for CK with $632M NPV and 27% IRR; $394M capex
feed.summary: U.S. Gold has released a Feasibility Study for its CK Gold Project, highlighting an impressive after-tax NPV(5%) of $632 million and a 27% IRR based on standard metal prices. The project is fully permitted for construction and boasts an initial 11-year mine plan with an average annual production of 85 koz AuEq. Initial capital expenditure is estimated at $394 million, with a quick payback period of approximately 2.5 years.

feed.title: Ford Says Its New Build Method Will Make Cars Cheaper to Fix. We're Not Buying It
feed.summary: Ford is implementing a new manufacturing method using large aluminum castings for vehicle structures, claiming it will lead to cheaper repairs after crashes. However, the author argues that these "gigacast" structures, while simplifying initial assembly, are difficult to repair in the field, often requiring full replacement of large, expensive components and potentially leading to higher total-loss rates for vehicles. The article expresses skepticism about Ford's repairability claims, suggesting the strategy is primarily driven by production cost savings and competitive pressures.

feed.title: CK Gold Feasibility Study shows strong economics for U.S. Gold Corp. (NASDAQ: USAU)
feed.summary: U.S. Gold Corp. announced strong Feasibility Study results for its wholly-owned CK Gold Project in Wyoming, outlining an 11-year open-pit mine with robust economics. The study projects an after-tax net present value of $632 million, a 27% internal rate of return, and a 2.5-year payback, with all necessary permits already in hand. The project is expected to produce 931 thousand gold-equivalent ounces and create 198 permanent jobs, with significant upside potential.

feed.title: Stock awards increase Huntington Bancshares (HBAN) director Teresa Shea’s holdings
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares director Teresa H. Shea received stock awards, not open-market purchases, as compensation on April 1, 2026. She was granted 199.648 shares of common stock directly and 95.678 shares credited to a Director Deferred Compensation Plan. Following these transactions, her direct holdings total 21,653.942 shares and indirect holdings through the deferred plan are 9,951.578 shares.

feed.title: Huntington Bancshares SVP granted 2,929.854 shares | HBAN Insider Trading
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares Senior Executive Vice President Brantley J. Standridge was granted 2,929.854 shares of common stock on April 1, 2026. This stock-based compensation, valued at $0.0000 per share, increased his direct holdings to 458,322.847 common shares. The transaction was classified as a grant or award, not a market purchase or sale.

feed.title: Director Ann B. Crane granted Huntington Bancshares (HBAN) stock units
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares Inc. director Ann B. Crane reported receiving routine equity compensation awards. On April 1, 2026, she acquired 1,385.713 directly held shares and 977.828 additional common stock units under a Director Deferred Compensation Plan, both at no cash cost. Following these grants, her direct holdings increased to 223,471.849 common shares, and indirect holdings through the deferred compensation plan rose to 101,704.882 shares-equivalent.

feed.title: Executive stock grant at Huntington Bancshares (HBAN) adds 1,814 shares
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares Inc. reported that SEVP-CHRO Sarah E. Pohmer received a grant of 1,814.4000 shares of Common Stock on April 1, 2026. Following this grant, Pohmer directly owns 186,873.3060 shares. This transaction was recorded as an acquisition with no purchase price.

feed.title: Huntington Bancshares (HBAN) operations chief gets 536-share stock grant
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares' Chief Corp Operations Officer, Prashant Nateri, received a grant of 536.5020 shares of common stock on April 1, 2026, at a price of $0.0000 per share. This transaction is a routine compensation-related equity award, not an open-market purchase. Following this grant, Nateri directly holds 84,355.3540 shares of Huntington Bancshares common stock.

feed.title: Huntington Bancshares (HBAN) general counsel receives 2.1K share grant
feed.summary: Marcy C. Hingst, Senior Executive Vice President and General Counsel of Huntington Bancshares (HBAN), received a grant of 2,120.633 common shares at no cost, which is a compensation award. This transaction increases her direct holdings to 278,427.194 shares. The Form 4 filing indicates this was a grant, not a market purchase or sale, providing transparency on executive compensation.

feed.title: [Form 4] HUNTINGTON BANCSHARES INC /MD/ Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares Inc. Senior Executive V.P., Scott D. Kleinman, reported acquisition of 2,085.568 common shares directly and 3.487 shares indirectly through stock awards, not open market purchases. These awards, valued at $0.00 per share, are routine compensation. After the transactions, Kleinman holds 483,761.185 direct and 362.563 indirect shares, with the filing including a standard disclaimer about beneficial ownership under Section 16.

feed.title: US Gold Corp's CK Gold Feasibility Study & Upside: 8 Things You Need to Know
feed.summary: US Gold Corp's CK Gold Project Feasibility Study outlines a robust unrisked after-tax NPV5% of $632 million at a base-case gold price of $3,250/ounce, rising to $1.3 billion at current spot prices. The project benefits from full state permitting, an 11-year mine life, and identified upside potential through resource expansion, improved gold recovery, and additional revenue streams from aggregate sales, which are not included in the base-case economics. The company is exploring financing options for the $394 million initial capital expenditure, with a construction decision anticipated in 2026.

feed.title: COCA COLA CO Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Bela Bajaria, a director at COCA COLA CO (KO), reported receiving 2,756.8922 phantom share units as part of her 2026 compensation under the company's Directors' Plan. These units, economically equivalent to one share of common stock, will be settled in cash after she leaves the Board. Following this grant, Bajaria's total phantom share unit holdings amount to 7,174.7809, with an additional 2,100 common shares indirectly held by a family trust.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Buys Innovator, Adds $31 Billion In Defined Outcome ETFs
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has acquired Innovator Capital Management, adding approximately $31 billion in assets under supervision and 171 ETFs to its portfolio. This acquisition positions Goldman among the top 10 global active ETF providers, significantly expanding its presence in the growing defined outcome ETF market. CEO David Solomon emphasized that the move enhances the firm's ability to offer tailored investment solutions with a focus on income generation, downside protection, and defined return profiles.

feed.title: [Form 4] COCA COLA CO Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Carolyn Everson, a director at COCA COLA CO (KO), reported the acquisition of 2,638.1743 phantom share units valued at $75.81 per unit, granted under The Coca-Cola Company Directors' Plan for 2026 compensation. These phantom units are economically equivalent to common stock but will be settled in cash upon her departure from the Board. Including credited phantom dividends, her total phantom share balance increased to 14,754.1151 units, in addition to her direct holding of 1,582 shares of common stock.

feed.title: Linde (LIN) director adds deferred and restricted stock unit holdings
feed.summary: Linde plc director Paula Rosput Reynolds has received a grant of 17.212 Deferred Stock Units, increasing her total to 128.887. She also holds 473 Restricted Stock Units which vest one year after March 9, 2026, and an additional 470.156 deferred Restricted Stock Units, alongside 715.922 direct Ordinary Shares. These equity awards are part of her compensation, convertible into Ordinary Shares on a one-for-one basis, and are subject to continuous Board service and terms of the Linde Non-Employee Director Deferral Plan.

feed.title: Linde (LIN) director Hugh Grant granted deferred and restricted stock units
feed.summary: Linde plc director Hugh Grant reported receiving a grant of 68.85 Deferred Stock Units and updated his holdings, which now include 515.551 Deferred Stock Units and 473 and 470.156 Restricted Stock Units. These units are convertible into ordinary shares on a one-for-one basis under the company's non-employee director deferral plan. One RSU award is set to vest one year after the March 9, 2026 grant date, provided continuous Board service, with payout deferred until his Board service ends.

feed.title: Costco Membership: The Recurring Revenue Powerhouse Driving Consistent Growth for North American Inv
feed.summary: Costco's membership model generates billions in stable, high-margin revenue with over 93% renewal rates, making it a critical asset for the company's resilience. This subscription-based approach mitigates retail sector volatility, fuels expansion, and positions Costco favorably for investors seeking defensive growth amid economic uncertainties. The model also supports consistent stock performance and enables investments without significant debt.

feed.title: Director Andrew B. Rose receives 700-share stock award at Trex (NYSE: TREX)
feed.summary: Trex director Andrew B. Rose was awarded 700 shares of the company's common stock on April 1, 2026, as a restricted stock unit (RSU) award. The shares were valued at $36.57 each for reporting purposes and will vest on the first anniversary of the grant. Following this transaction, Rose directly holds 14,883 shares of Trex common stock.

feed.title: Why Turning Point Brands Stock Fell 15.5% This Week
feed.summary: Turning Point Brands (TPB) stock fell 15.5% this week due to reports of slower Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approvals for nicotine pouches, a fast-growing segment for the company. The FDA's hesitation stems from concerns over unknown health impacts and use among younger individuals, reminiscent of the vaping epidemic. Despite the company's stock being down significantly from its highs, its diverse revenue streams from rolling papers, chewing tobacco, and nicotine pouches could make it an attractive buy for investors who see the FDA issues as a temporary setback.

feed.title: Public Storage (PSA) Announces $500 Million Senior Notes Offering
feed.summary: Public Storage (PSA) has announced an offering of $500 million in senior notes due 2035, bearing an annual interest rate of 5.000%. The proceeds will be used to repay existing credit facilities and fund general corporate purposes, including investments in self-storage facilities and debt repayment. This strategic move aims to strengthen the company's financial position and support its growth initiatives.

feed.title: Elevance expands out-of-network referral penalties to New York
feed.summary: Elevance Health is extending its out-of-network referral penalties to New York as of April 3, 2026, at 12:03 PM CDT. This move by the insurance giant indicates a broader application of its policies regarding healthcare providers outside its network. The article highlights the expansion of these penalties, impacting healthcare access and costs for New York residents with Elevance Health plans.

feed.title: BLACKROCK TCP DEADLINE MONDAY: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C.
feed.summary: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. has filed a class action lawsuit against BlackRock TCP Capital Corp. (NASDAQ: TCPC) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between November 6, 2024, and January 23, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that BlackRock TCP made misleading statements regarding its investments, portfolio restructuring, and valuation, leading to an overstatement of its Net Asset Value. Investors who suffered losses are encouraged to contact the firm before the April 6, 2026, deadline to apply for lead plaintiff.

feed.title: Dana Williams retires from Parker-Hannifin
feed.summary: Dana Williams has retired from Parker-Hannifin after 30 years with the company, 16 of which he served as plant manager. His colleagues presented him with a signed guitar, acknowledging his passion for music. Williams plans to travel, play music, and ride bikes with his wife, Crisi, who also plans to retire this year.

feed.title: Rollins: Pest Control Company Acquires Romex Pest Control To Expand U.S. Footprint
feed.summary: Rollins, a global pest control provider, has acquired Romex Pest Control, a rapidly growing company based in Utah. This acquisition allows Rollins to expand into new regional markets while Romex will continue to operate under its existing brand. The move aligns with Rollins' strategy to acquire high-quality businesses in the fragmented pest control industry.

feed.title: American Electric Power’s $72 Billion Capital Plan Just Got Bigger: Why It Could Set New Highs in 2026
feed.summary: American Electric Power (AEP) has significantly expanded its contracted load pipeline to 56 gigawatts by 2030, driven by the increasing demand from AI infrastructure. This expansion bolsters its $72 billion five-year capital plan and new incremental projects, strengthening its position as a primary grid contractor for the AI era. TIKR's analysis suggests this growth could lead to a mid-case normalized EPS of $8.92 by 2030 and a target stock price of $180.62.

feed.title: Public Storage (PSA) director delivers 266 shares to cover tax liability
feed.summary: Public Storage director Ronald L. Havner Jr. reported a disposition of 266 common shares at $276.24 per share on April 1, 2026, to cover tax liabilities related to equity compensation. Following this transaction, he directly holds 7,460.59 common shares and indirectly owns 317,053 shares through the Havner Family Trust and 1,900 shares via his spouse's IRA. The trust's holdings include deferred share units from a 2015 restricted share unit grant, with 4,000 RSUs remaining subject to deferred receipt through 2030.

feed.title: Ubiquiti (UI) Is Up 8.1% After Volatile Rebound From 52-Week Low Has The Bull Case Changed?
feed.summary: Ubiquiti's (UI) stock has seen significant volatility, rebounding from a 52-week low to trade above its 30-day moving average, despite mixed valuation assessments. The company is characterized by a capital-light networking business model, strong margins, no debt, and improving profitability, paying regular dividends. However, investor focus is drawn to the interplay between its strong financials, recent price swings, mixed fair value estimates, and ongoing governance concerns.

feed.title: Bloomia Holdings Completes $12.1M Rights Offering
feed.summary: Bloomia Holdings, a specialty agriculture company, has successfully completed a $12.1 million rights offering. This offering involved the issuance of 3 million new shares at $4.05 each, raising $5 million in cash and converting $7.1 million of debt. The capital raised will be used to reduce acquisition debt, lower interest expenses, and strengthen the company's financial position for future growth in tulip production.

feed.title: Amgen Expands Thyroid Eye Disease Bet With New AMG 732 Rollover Study
feed.summary: Amgen Inc. (AMGN) has announced a new Phase 2, open-label rollover study for its drug AMG 732, targeting patients with Thyroid Eye Disease (TED) who previously did not respond to treatment or relapsed. This study focuses on tougher cases to gather relevant real-world data on the drug's efficacy and safety for individuals with high unmet needs. Amgen's move indicates a strategy to solidify its position in the TED market by addressing difficult-to-treat patient populations, which could lead to a broader drug label and positive investor sentiment.

feed.title: Savers Value Village to open first store in western Pennsylvania
feed.summary: Savers Value Village, a national thrift store chain, is set to open its first location in western Pennsylvania. The store will be located in the Great Southern Shopping Center and works with nonprofits to source its merchandise.

feed.title: A Look At Applied Materials (AMAT) Valuation After Earnings Beat AI Demand Tailwinds And Regulatory Resolution
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) has reported strong earnings and guidance, projecting over 20% growth in its semiconductor equipment business for 2026, and resolved a regulatory investigation. Despite a significant share price increase over the past year, Simply Wall St's narrative valuation suggests AMAT is overvalued at $194.11 compared to its current price of $348.47, highlighting a tension between ambitious growth assumptions and underlying risks like China export restrictions. However, its P/E ratio is similar to the industry average, suggesting the market isn't overly stretched.

feed.title: Chubb Ltd stock: Steady performer in volatile insurance markets?
feed.summary: The article analyzes Chubb Ltd (CB) as a potentially stable investment in the volatile insurance sector, highlighting its global presence, diversified offerings, and disciplined underwriting as key strengths. It suggests that Chubb's focus on specialty lines and strong financial performance makes it an attractive option for North American investors seeking defensive growth and consistent returns. The piece also weighs potential risks such as intense competition and rising catastrophe costs, while noting a generally positive outlook from analysts.

feed.title: Sony Group Corporation's PlayStation 5: Enduring Gaming Dominance and Strategic Evolution in a Volat
feed.summary: Amidst a turbulent global market in 2026, Sony's PlayStation 5 continues to drive significant revenue through exclusive titles, hardware innovations, and an expanding digital ecosystem, appealing strongly to North American investors. The console, with over 65 million units sold, maintains a dominant market share and is strategically shifting towards recurring revenue models through live-service games and its PlayStation Plus service. Sony is also looking towards future growth with PSVR2, upcoming PS6 development, and metaverse initiatives, positioning gaming as a stable and profitable division within the company.

feed.title: Bronx-based Ponce Bank lands in S&P's top 50 U.S. community banks
feed.summary: Ponce Bank (PDLB) has been recognized by S&P Global Market Intelligence as number 40 among the top 50 best-performing U.S. community banks for 2025 in the $3 billion–$10 billion asset category. This recognition highlights the Bronx-based bank's strong financial performance, its commitment to community impact and inclusive growth, and a balanced approach to risk and long-term stability. Ponce Bank, a Minority Depository Institution (MDI) and Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI), will be formally recognized at the S&P Community Bankers Conference in May 2026.

feed.title: SCHMID Group N.V. Insider Trading & Ownership Structure
feed.summary: This article details the insider trading and ownership structure of SCHMID Group N.V. (NasdaqGM:SHMD). It highlights a significant insider sale by Christian Schmid and provides a breakdown of the company's ownership by different entity types, including institutions, individual insiders, VC/PE firms, the general public, and private companies. The report also lists the top 25 shareholders and notes a 29.4% dilution of shares over the past year.

feed.title: Select Water (NYSE: WTTR) SVP awarded 49,690 restricted shares vesting 2027–2029
feed.summary: Select Water Solutions, Inc. (NYSE: WTTR) SVP, General Counsel & CCO Robert Allen Wilson was granted 49,690 restricted shares of Class A Common Stock. These shares were awarded as compensation rather than purchased on the open market. The shares will vest in tranches, with 24,138 shares vesting on April 1, 2028, and the remaining 25,552 shares vesting in thirds between April 1, 2027, and April 1, 2029, under the company's 2024 Equity Incentive Plan.

feed.title: Silvercrest Asset Management Group (NasdaqGM:SAMG) Stock Forecast & Analyst Predictions
feed.summary: Silvercrest Asset Management Group (SAMG) is forecast to grow earnings by 26.7% and revenue by 5.8% annually, outperforming the US market's earnings growth but lagging its revenue growth. The company has a low forecast Return on Equity of 16.4% in three years. Recent updates include increased dividends and analyst price targets, alongside various executive appointments and reporting of quarterly results.

feed.title: Valvoline (VVV) CEO Lori Flees adds deferred stock units via salary deferral
feed.summary: Valvoline Inc. CEO Lori Ann Flees has acquired 29 deferred stock units through a salary deferral under the company's 2016 Deferred Compensation Plan. This transaction increases her total direct holdings to 15,412 deferred stock units. These units represent a contingent right to receive Valvoline common stock upon specific events such as an unforeseeable emergency or separation from service, and are part of her compensation rather than an open-market purchase.
